A Discounted Cash Flow model takes projected future cash flows, then discounts them back to today to estimate what the business might be worth right now. It is essentially asking what those future dollars are worth in today's terms.

For Kinross Gold, the latest reported free cash flow is about $2.22b. The current DCF uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model based on cash flow projections, with analyst inputs for earlier years and extrapolated figures beyond that. By 2030, free cash flow is projected at $2.48b, with a series of annual projections between 2026 and 2035 that gradually adjust from analyst based estimates to modelled extensions.

On this basis, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of $48.07 per share. Compared with the current share price of around $38.49, the DCF output points to Kinross Gold trading at roughly a 19.9% discount, which indicates that the shares may be undervalued according to these cash flow assumptions.

What counts as a "normal" or "fair" P/E usually reflects how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk tends to justify a lower P/E.

Regulatory green light for Rook I shifts focus to NexGen’s construction phase

NexGen Energy (TSX:NXE) received final federal approval for its 100%-owned Rook I Uranium Project, with Canada’s nuclear regulator granting environmental assessment clearance and a Licence to Prepare Site and Construct.

The decision allows NexGen to proceed toward full construction, which the company plans to start in summer 2026. This marks a new phase for investors watching the stock and its uranium development timetable.

See our latest analysis for NexGen Energy.

At a share price of CA$15.96, NexGen has a 90-day share price return of 23.43% and a 1-year total shareholder return of 124.79%, suggesting momentum has been building as Rook I moves through key approvals and the company refreshes its auditor.

With NexGen now holding a key federal approval, a CA$10.3b market value and a 1 year total return above 100%, the big question is whether today’s price underestimates Rook I’s potential or already reflects an extended period of future growth.

Price to book of 5.8x: is it justified?

On a P/B of 5.8x, NexGen trades well above the wider Canadian Oil and Gas industry average of 1.9x, which points to a premium valuation at the current CA$15.96 share price.

P/B compares the market value of a company to its net assets, so it is often used for asset heavy sectors such as resources. With NexGen still loss making, traditional earnings based ratios are less useful, which puts more attention on how much investors are ready to pay for its balance sheet and development assets.

The premium to the sector average suggests the market is accepting a higher multiple of NexGen’s book value than for many peers, even though NexGen remains unprofitable, has reported a loss of CA$309.676m and is forecast to be unprofitable and have no revenue next year. Against that, the stock’s P/B of 5.8x sits below the peer group average of 7.4x, which implies investors are still pricing it below some closest comparables despite the broader sector gap.

data.title: New Recursion CMO Puts Oncology Experience At Center Of Investor Focus

  • Her background includes senior roles at Mural Oncology, Exelixis, Merck, Bristol Myers Squibb, and GlaxoSmithKline, with experience on major oncology products such as KEYTRUDA, OPDIVO, and YERVOY.

For investors tracking Recursion Pharmaceuticals at a share price of $3.17, this leadership change follows a period of weak share price returns. The stock has declined 24.5% year to date and 51.5% over the past year, with a 50.6% decline over three years, highlighting execution risk for a clinical stage biotechnology company.

Dr. Goodman’s appointment increases focus on how Recursion progresses its clinical pipeline and interacts with regulators from April 2026 onward. Investors may want to monitor future updates on trial progress, partnership activity, and any regulatory filings to observe how this leadership change affects the company’s path.

Vicki Goodman’s track record across Merck, Bristol Myers Squibb, Exelixis and Mural Oncology gives Recursion a leader who has worked on globally marketed oncology drugs and managed complex late stage programs. For you as an investor, the key question is whether that experience translates into tighter trial design, clearer regulatory dialogue and more efficient resource allocation across Recursion’s oncology and rare disease assets. Leadership changes at this level can also influence how a company partners with larger drug makers such as Merck, Roche, or Sanofi, especially if the CMO is well known in oncology circles. At the same time, a handover after roughly three years means there is a period of adjustment for internal teams and external partners, and investors will be watching closely for continuity of clinical strategy under the new CMO.

How This Fits Into The Recursion Pharmaceuticals Narrative

  • This appointment supports the narrative that Recursion wants to push its internal oncology programs toward clinical inflection points by bringing in a CMO who has overseen late stage trials and approvals for checkpoint inhibitors and targeted therapies.
  • It may challenge assumptions that Recursion can lean heavily on its AI driven Recursion OS alone, since the company is clearly prioritising traditional drug development experience alongside its technology platform.
  • The prior narrative focused mainly on data platforms, partnerships and valuation targets, and did not fully account for how a leadership change at CMO level could influence trial design, regulatory risk and timelines.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Recursion Pharmaceuticals to help decide what it's worth to you.

  • 🎁 Dr. Goodman’s experience across KEYTRUDA, OPDIVO, YERVOY and other oncology assets may help Recursion frame its trial designs and regulatory packages in ways that align with FDA expectations.
  • 🎁 A seasoned CMO with both biotech and large pharma experience can strengthen Recursion’s credibility when discussing partnerships or potential future indications, which may support its collaboration based business model.

From here, focus on how Recursion updates timelines and study designs for its key oncology and rare disease programs once Dr. Goodman is in place. Watch for any changes in trial initiation or readout timing, shifts in how the company talks about regulatory paths and whether partner announcements reference her involvement. For a stock that has seen a 51.5% 1 year share price decline, investors will likely look for evidence that this leadership change contributes to clearer clinical progress and disciplined capital use rather than further restructuring.

data.description:

How United Laboratories International Holdings' Latest Numbers Set the Stage for the Narrative

United Laboratories International Holdings (SEHK:3933) has put up a busy FY 2025 first half, with revenue of about C¥7.5b and basic EPS of C¥1.04 sitting against trailing twelve month revenue of roughly C¥14.1b and basic EPS of C¥1.69. Investors are sifting through what the shift in earnings power really means. Over the last three reported half year periods, revenue has moved from C¥7.2b in 1H 2024 to C¥6.6b in 2H 2024 and then C¥7.5b in 1H 2025, while basic EPS has gone from C¥0.82 to C¥0.64 and then C¥1.04. This sets up a results season where the focus falls squarely on how much of that earnings profile can hold up if margins stay under pressure.

See our full analysis for United Laboratories International Holdings.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this mix of revenue, EPS and margin trends lines up with the big narratives investors already have in mind about United Laboratories International Holdings.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Margins Under Pressure, Even As Profit Holds Up

  • Trailing net profit margin moved from 19.3% to 15.8% over the last year, while trailing twelve month net income excluding extra items was C¥3.1b on about C¥14.1b of revenue.
  • What bears focus on is that margin compression and weaker free cash flow coverage of the 6.19% dividend align with a cautious view, yet:
    • Net income excluding extra items over the last three reported half years was C¥1.5b, C¥1.2b and then C¥1.9b, which sits alongside the 15.8% trailing margin rather than pointing to a collapse in profitability.
    • This challenges a very bearish take that margins are unraveling entirely, while still backing the concern that current dividend payouts will likely stay under scrutiny until cash flows and margins are better aligned.

Valuation Gap Versus Peers And DCF Fair Value

  • The company trades on a trailing P/E of 8.6x compared with 11.5x for the Hong Kong pharmaceuticals industry and 23.7x for peers, while a DCF fair value of HK$26.03 sits well above the current HK$10.27 share price.
  • Supporters of a bullish view point to this valuation discount and earnings record as a potential upside setup, but the numbers cut both ways:
    • The gap between HK$10.27 and the HK$26.03 DCF fair value, together with mid double digit annual earnings growth over five years, is often cited in support of the bullish case that the stock is priced below what those models imply.
    • At the same time, revenue growth running at about 5.9% compared with a Hong Kong market average of 8.2% highlights why some investors question how quickly that valuation gap might close, even if the discount looks large on paper.

Some investors want to see how this valuation picture fits into a wider story of growth, risk and expectations before making their own call, so it can help to step back and see how others are framing the trade through different market narratives. Earnings Trend Steadier Than Revenue Growth

  • Over the last three half year periods, revenue moved from C¥7.2b to C¥6.6b and then to C¥7.5b, while basic EPS across the same halves came in at C¥0.82, C¥0.64 and then C¥1.04.
  • Supporters of a bullish stance often highlight that pattern as evidence that the business has held its earnings profile even while top line growth trailed the Hong Kong market, which the figures partly support:
